source_evidence: Section 3.4: 'the user defines restriction zones (i.e. hitboxes): geometric entities that no bar should intersect.' Primitives: box, cylinder, disc, quadrangle (flat), rod (finite cylinder), sphere, triangle (flat), surface. 'all bars originating from such node will be flagged for removal during the restriction zone check.' A setback tol is advised.
purpose: Removes candidate bars that intersect user-defined restriction primitives (holes, concavities, barriers), so the ground structure lays no members in forbidden regions of the domain.
assumptions: Nodes in the base mesh must not intersect the restriction zones nor their boundary; otherwise all bars from such a node are flagged for removal
A small setback margin tol (relative to problem scale) keeps concave-boundary nodes clear of the restriction primitive
Restriction zones are typically a subset of the primitives used to construct the domain
Primitives may be combined to form complicated regions/boundaries
formula_or_algorithm: After ground structure generation, for each candidate bar (segment) test intersection against each restriction primitive using the segment-vs-primitive collision tests (Appendix B: box, triangle, quadrangle, sphere, disc, cylinder, rod, surface). If a bar intersects any primitive it is removed. Apply after or alongside the collinearity check; return the list of accepted bars (only p<q reported).
validation_opportunities: unit test per primitive with known intersecting/non-intersecting segments
benchmark on diamond (disc restriction), cup (rod restriction), crane (box restriction), Lotte tower (surface restriction) examples

reuse_risk.explanation: Robust in principle but depends on correct, numerically stable collision primitives and appropriate setback tol; boundary/edge cases and floating-point precision can cause missed or spurious removals.

missing_information: Guidance for choosing tol per problem scale
Order of application relative to collinearity check in code
How combined primitives are logically unioned/intersected
